entities.name: CONTINGENT VALUATION
entities.description: A survey-based economic valuation method used to elicit willingness to pay for a service or tool.

entities.name: WILLINGNESS TO PAY
entities.description: The maximum amount that irrigators are willing to spend for soil moisture monitoring tools.

entities.name: SOIL MOISTURE MONITORING TOOLS
entities.description: Tools used to monitor the moisture levels in soil for irrigation management.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The continent where the irrigation schemes are located.

entities.name: SOUTH-EASTERN AFRICA
entities.description: The specific region in Africa where the study is focused.

entities.name: ECONOMETRIC METHODS
entities.description: Statistical methods used to analyze economic data and mitigate potential bias.

entities.name: NEIGHBOURHOOD EFFECT
entities.description: The influence of nearby farmers' behavior on the adoption of tools.

entities.name: ADOPTION
entities.description: The act of starting to use soil moisture monitoring tools.

entities.name: DOWNSTREAM LOCATION
entities.description: The position of a farm relative to the water flow in an irrigation scheme.

entities.name: IRRIGATION WATER EXPENDITURE
entities.description: The amount of money spent by farmers on irrigation water.

entities.name: ECONOMIC INCENTIVES
entities.description: Financial motivations that may encourage the adoption of tools.

entities.name: FARMER LEARNING
entities.description: The process of farmers acquiring knowledge from others who use the tools.

entities.name: CO-INVESTMENT
entities.description: Financial contribution from multiple bodies to support the adoption of tools.
